  "sound_source": "loose termination or failing circuit breaker causing electrical arcing/micro-arcing",
  "confidence": 0.95,

  "findings": [
    "Loud, persistent 120 Hz electrical hum/buzzing indicating a loose connection or failing component under load",
    "Severe risk of thermal degradation, localized overheating, and potential electrical fire"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate power to the panel immediately",
    "Perform a thermal imaging scan and physical torque check on all terminations",
    "Inspect breaker contacts and busbar for signs of pitting, arcing, or heat damage",
    "Re-terminate or replace damaged components in accordance with manufacturer specifications"
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio recording exhibits a strong, continuous 120 Hz hum characteristic of an electrical fault, such as a loose termination or failing contactor/breaker under load. This condition presents an immediate fire and safety hazard, requiring isolation and corrective maintenance.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14 (Electrical Connections)",
    "NEC 110.12 (Mechanical Execution of Work)",
    "NFPA 70B (Recommended Practice for Electrical Equipment Maintenance)"
  ]
}
